subject: Re: Start from ts_import
body:
Also - it's probably best to run through the tutorial yourself to see what
inputs/outputs look like

On Mon, 27 Jan 2025 at 13:11, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Luis,
>
> Please see
> https://warpem.github.io/warp/reference/warptools/processing_tilt_series_stacks for
> some tips
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On Mon, 27 Jan 2025 at 11:46, Luis Carreira <luismenezescarreira@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Hi all,
>>
>> I am starting to use WarpTools and would like to know the following: I
>> have a few tilt series stacks already motion corrected. Is it possible to
>> use ts_import to upload these? I have tried the command
>>
>> WarpTools ts_import \
>> --mdocs /Warptest/ \
>> --frameseries warp_frameseries \
>> --tilt_exposure 2.26324 \
>> --output tomostar \
>> --dont_invert
>>
>> And it spits out the following:
>>
>> Found 3 MDOC files, searching for *.mdoc in /Warptest/
>> Looking for frame series...
>> Unhandled exception. System.Exception: No frame series metadata found at
>> /Warptest/warp_frameseries
>>    at WarpTools.Commands.ImportTiltseries.Run(Object options) in
>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1737588682413/work/WarpTools/Commands/Tiltseries/ImportTiltseries.cs:line
>> 118
>>    at WarpTools.WarpTools.Run(Object options) in
>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1737588682413/work/WarpTools/Program.cs:line
>> 30
>>    at Warp.Tools.CommandLineParserHelper.ParseAndRun(String[] args,
>> Func`2 run, Type[] verbs, String appName) in
>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1737588682413/work/WarpLib/Tools/CommandLineParserHelper.cs:line
>> 26
>>    at WarpTools.WarpTools.Main(String[] args) in
>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1737588682413/work/WarpTools/Program.cs:line
>> 17
>>    at WarpTools.WarpTools.<Main>(String[] args)
>> /script/run_warp: line 16: 47738 Aborted                 WarpTools
>> ts_import --mdocs /Warptest/ --frameseries warp_frameseries --tilt_exposure
>> 2.26324 --output tomostar.
>>
>> Which kind of metadata is required?
>>
>> Thanks,
>> Luis
>>
>>
>> --
>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups
>> "Warp" group.
>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an
>> email to warp-em+unsubscribe@googlegroups.com.
>> To view this discussion visit
>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/9d315c32-b631-4954-b1ef-e6110d0e5669n%40googlegroups.com
>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/9d315c32-b631-4954-b1ef-e6110d0e5669n%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>> .
>>
>
